_comment: Private Health Insurance Rebate — FY 2025-26 income thresholds and rebate percentages
_note: Rebate percentages are indexed on 1 April each year. FY 2025-26 has TWO rebate periods: 1 Jul 2025 – 31 Mar 2026, and 1 Apr 2026 – 30 Jun 2026. The income thresholds are the same for both periods (set on 1 July). For the calculator's default display, use Period 1 rates (9 of 12 months).

incomeThresholds._comment: Same thresholds as MLS — set on 1 July each financial year

under65.label: Under 65
under65.description: Aged under 65 on 1 July of the financial year

age65to69.label: 65 to 69
age65to69.description: Aged 65 to 69 on 1 July of the financial year

age70plus.label: 70 or over
age70plus.description: Aged 70 or over on 1 July of the financial year

rebateRules.claimMethod: You can claim the rebate as a premium reduction through your insurer (most common) or as a tax offset when you lodge your tax return
rebateRules.coupleAgeRule: If you are a couple and one of you is aged 65 or older, the rebate for both of you is based on the older person's age bracket
rebateRules.incomeDefinition: Same as MLS income — taxable income + reportable fringe benefits + total net investment losses + reportable super contributions (+ trust/foreign income if applicable)
rebateRules.updateCycle: Rebate percentages are indexed on 1 April each year. Income thresholds are indexed on 1 July each year. This means within a single financial year, two different rebate percentage sets may apply.
rebateRules.tier3Note: Tier 3 income earners receive 0% rebate regardless of age. They pay the full premium with no government contribution.
